summary: Conduct legal due diligence from document collection through risk flagging to attorney review.

inputs.name: diligence_scope
inputs.description: Areas of legal diligence to conduct.

inputs.name: document_inventory
inputs.description: Available documents and data room contents.

outputs.name: diligence_report
outputs.description: Structured findings organized by diligence category.

outputs.name: risk_flags
outputs.description: Identified risks with severity and recommended actions.

outputs.name: attorney_review_packet
outputs.description: Summary with key findings for attorney decision-making.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is due diligence and not legal opinion on transaction viability.
Require transaction type and diligence scope.
Flag risks without asserting definitive conclusions on deal viability.
End with attorney review packet; do not recommend for or against the transaction.

steps.action: Define diligence scope and organize document inventory.
Review material contracts for risks and obligations.
Assess IP portfolio completeness and protection gaps.
Review employment agreements and workplace compliance.
Merge findings into diligence report with attorney handoff.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read documents, policies, and reference materials.

verification.method: Check the output defines transaction type and diligence scope.
Check each risk includes category, severity, and recommended action.
Check output includes attorney review packet with key findings.

success_criteria: Diligence scope covers all required categories.
Each risk flag includes severity, rationale, and next steps.
Output ends with attorney review packet, not deal recommendations.

rollback.when: If scope, confidentiality, or review authority is unclear, revert the draft and regenerate a narrower output for counsel review.
